author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
The Old Testament is Properly One Thing—The Old Instrument Another.
title: The Old Testament is Properly One Thing—The Old Instrument Another.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Therefore, by a custom of speech already prevailing, in one way the law and all the prophets who prophesied until John are called the “Old Testament;” although this is more definitely called the “Old Instrument” rather than the “Old Testament;” but this name is used in another way by the apostolical authority, whether expressly or impliedly.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: For the apostle is express when he says, “Until this day, as long as Moses is read, remaineth the same veil in the reading of the old testament; because it is not revealed, because it is made of no effect in Christ.”

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: For thus certainly the old testament referred to the ministry of Moses.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Moreover, he says, “That we should serve in the newness of the Spirit, and not in the oldness of the letter,” signifying that same testament under the name of the letter.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: In another place also, “Who also hath made us able ministers of the new testament; not of the letter, but of the Spirit: for the letter killeth, but the Spirit maketh alive.”

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: And here, by the mention of the new, he certainly meant the former to be understood as the old.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: But much more evidently, although he did not say either old or new, he distinguished the two testaments and the two sons of Abraham, the one of the bondwoman, the other of the free, as I have above mentioned.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: For what can be more express than his saying, “Tell me, ye that desire to be under the law, have ye not heard the law?

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: For it is written, that Abraham had two sons, the one by a bondmaid, the other by a freewoman.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: But he who was of the bondwoman was born after the flesh; but he of the freewoman was by promise.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Which things are in allegory; for these are the two testaments; the one in the Mount Sinai, gendering to bondage, which is Agar.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: For Sinai is a mountain in Arabia, which is associated with Jerusalem which now is, for it is in bondage with her children.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: But Jerusalem that is above is free, which is our mother?”

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: What is more clear, what more certain, what more remote from all obscurity and ambiguity to the children of the promise?

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: And a little after, “Now we, brethren, as Isaac was, are the children of promise.”

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Also a little after, “But we, brethren, are not children of the bondwoman, but of the free,” with the liberty with which Christ has made us free.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Let us, therefore, choose whether to call the righteous men of old the children of the bondwoman or of the free.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Be it far from us to say, of the bondwoman; therefore if of the free, they pertain to the new testament in the Holy Spirit, whom, as making alive, the apostle opposes to the killing letter.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: For on what ground do they not belong to the grace of the new testament, from whose words and looks we convict and rebut such most frantic and ungrateful enemies of the same grace as these?
